era: Hip-Hop. E=5 -- the combined energy of Filipino-American identity, Blue Scholars legacy, and politically charged solo artistry. Self-released / independent. Prometheus Brown (George Quibuyen), also known as Geologic, is one half of Blue Scholars (degree 99, return at 156). Solo work explores Filipino-American identity. Nineteenth degree in the hip-hop era (134-152).

location.formative: Filipino-American community. Beacon Hill and International District. Filipino immigration history -- cannery workers, agricultural laborers, 1965 Immigration Act. Colonial legacy (US colony 1898-1946). Manong generation.
location.career: Prometheus Brown / Geologic / George Quibuyen. Solo MC. Filipino-American hip-hop. Blue Scholars connection. Community organizer. Visual artist. Artist-activist.

members.name: Prometheus Brown (George Quibuyen / Geologic)
members.note: Filipino-American. Blue Scholars MC. Solo = Prometheus Brown. Duo = Geologic. Community organizer. Artist-activist. Tagalog/English code-switching.

associated_musicians.name: Blue Scholars (Sabzi)
associated_musicians.note: Blue Scholars at 99. Prometheus Brown solo at 152. Blue Scholars return at 156. Solo entry fills the gap.

associated_musicians.name: Filipino-American artists
associated_musicians.note: Features from other Filipino-American artists, community members, elders. Music embedded in community.

associated_musicians.name: Gabriel Teodros
associated_musicians.note: Diaspora parallel. Ethiopian-American (Teodros) and Filipino-American (Prometheus Brown). Both exploring identity through hip-hop.

theory_nodes.concept: Filipino-American identity in Seattle -- one of the oldest and largest Filipino-American communities in the US; cannery workers, agricultural laborers, 1965 Immigration Act; Beacon Hill, International District; colonial legacy (US colony 1898-1946); Tagalog/English code-switching; manong generation
examples: Filipino immigration to PNW: cannery workers (Alaska), agricultural laborers (Yakima).
Beacon Hill and International District: Filipino businesses, churches, community orgs.
Colonial legacy: Philippines as US colony 1898-1946. English language from colonialism.
Tagalog/English code-switching in lyrics. Bilingual as daily reality.
theory_nodes.description: Filipino-American identity. Colonial legacy. Beacon Hill. Manong generation.

genealogy.relationship: Prometheus Brown at 152. Filipino-American identity documented.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue Scholars MC between appearances -- solo entry at 152 fills the gap between Blue Scholars debut (99) and return (156); documents the individual MC's voice separated from the duo; name multiplicity (George Quibuyen / Geologic / Prometheus Brown) reflects identity complexity
examples: Blue Scholars at 99: duo debut. Sabzi's production. Political hip-hop.
Prometheus Brown at 152: solo MC. Filipino-American identity. 53 degrees later.
Blue Scholars return at 156: duo reunited. 4 degrees after solo.
Three names: George Quibuyen (person), Geologic (duo MC), Prometheus Brown (solo artist).
theory_nodes.description: MC between duo appearances. Solo voice. Name multiplicity.

genealogy.relationship: Prometheus Brown at 152. Solo MC documented between duo appearances.

theory_nodes.concept: Artist-activist model -- art and activism inseparable; Carlos Bulosan tradition; community organizing + artistic practice as one discipline; education, youth workshops, benefit albums, cultural preservation
examples: Carlos Bulosan tradition: *America Is in the Heart* (1946). Filipino-American activist literature.
Community organizing: Filipino Community of Seattle, labor solidarity, arts orgs.
Education: youth workshops, university lectures, arts education.
Benefit albums, collaborative mixtapes, cultural event soundtracks. Art serves community.
theory_nodes.description: Artist-activist model. Art and activism inseparable.

cross_references.relationship: DUO CONNECTION! Blue Scholars debut at 99. Prometheus Brown solo at 152 (53 degrees later). Blue Scholars return at 156. The solo entry documents the individual voice.
DIASPORA PARALLEL! Ethiopian-American (Teodros, 148) and Filipino-American (Prometheus Brown, 152). Both exploring diaspora identity through hip-hop. Both navigating dual cultures.
PREVIOUS DEGREE! Conscious positivity (Sol, 151) to political fire (Prometheus Brown, 152). New generation to activist tradition. Different approaches to engaged hip-hop.
QUARTET BOOKEND! J. Pinder opens the hip-hop underground cluster (149). Prometheus Brown closes it (152). Four degrees, four MCs, the cluster complete.
